rows.source_ref: Babypips forum thread "My ultimate system - 128K% profit in 1 year" by user KGMK, 2024, https://forums.babypips.com/t/my-ultimate-system-128k-profit-in-1-year/1259295
rows.concept: Enter against a bar that closes beyond the prior bar's high/low and hold with no stop-loss until price reverts to a recent high/low ('take profit at a later date').
rows.eligibility_reason: Reported 100% win / 128K% stats are a look-ahead / no-stop-loss artifact (positions held indefinitely, author refuses SL, admits -300% drawdowns and a code bug); edge vanishes the moment any stop is added, so it is un-tradeable and incompatible with FTMO/DXZ DD limits.
rows.notes: Author flip-flops between gap-continuation (post #1: buy if open>prior open, TP=prior high) and mean-reversion (post #17/#22: sell if close>prior high, TP=recent low) - same fabricated no-stop core, one row. Demo/backtest only, never live (posts #66/#71); ML-optimised in later posts (#77 'put into machine learning'). ProfesorPips and others correctly call it overfit.

rows.source_ref: Babypips forum thread "My ultimate system - 128K% profit in 1 year", post #29 by user tommor, 2024, https://forums.babypips.com/t/my-ultimate-system-128k-profit-in-1-year/1259295
rows.concept: In an up-trend (Close > 50EMA) buy the break of the day's High with the day's Low as stop-loss, exiting at the first profitable close.
rows.eligibility_reason: Deterministic D1 breakout with a defined stop (day's Low) and exit (first profitable Close); pure-MQL5 implementable, DXZ FX data available, FTMO/DXZ DD-compatible.
rows.notes: Forum sketch by a different user (tommor, #29), untested in-thread and longs-only (symmetrical short rule to be defined). References Larry Williams 'Smash Day' (#27). Pyramiding ('do not cancel untriggered trades, keep adding new buy orders') needs a total-risk cap. Just-another-D1-breakout, hence LOW.

notes: Thread is 82 posts of one user (KGMK) posting fabricated no-stop backtest fantasy; only a single-line breakout idea from another user (tommor) is salvageable.
